scope: Compact reference for the original browser-side normal MoonCat rescue/mining operation, a reusable wallet-free example snippet, and an embeddable wallet-free widget example.

searchSeed.notes: The reviewed browser page passes this value into createMiner(searchSeed).

candidateSeed.generationObserved: random 32-byte array using Math.random in the reviewed browser worker
candidateSeed.notes: No sequential increment loop was observed in the reviewed browser worker; it repeatedly generates random candidates.

hashOperation.inputEncoding: concatenated 32-byte seed candidate followed by 32-byte searchSeed

validCatCheck.meaning: The first three bytes of the hash are zero.
validCatCheck.contractCrossCheck: data/protocol-constants.json records the same normal-cat valid-hash rule from the original MoonCatRescue Solidity source.

catIdDerivation.notes: The leading 0x00 byte marks the normal, non-genesis catId form in this context.

rescueCallBoundary.notes: The reusable example intentionally stops before this transaction boundary and does not include wallet, provider, account, or transaction code.

dependencyNotes: Caller must provide an Ethereum-compatible keccak256 implementation.
The keccak function should hash raw bytes equivalent to Solidity packed bytes32 seed plus bytes32 searchSeed.
Libraries such as js-sha3, ethers, or viem can be adapted by passing a small wrapper.
The widget vendors the original site's public/js/sha3.min.js at examples/rescue-mining-widget/vendor/sha3.min.js.
The vendored sha3.min.js header identifies js-sha3 0.6.1 by Chen, Yi-Cyuan under MIT and is preserved.
Hash provider order is options.keccak256(bytes), global js-sha3 keccak_256, then the internal Ethereum Keccak-256 fallback.
Each hash provider path hashes raw bytes equivalent to seedBytes || searchSeedBytes.
No Web3 dependency, worker file, parser code, image asset, remote asset, or unrelated frontend code is imported.

difficulty.defaultBehavior: Original reviewed rescue-mining behavior; valid when the hash starts with 000000.
difficulty.demoBehavior: Visible widget control and options.difficultyPrefix support faster local UI testing.
difficulty.demoLimitation: Demo mode is not original rescue difficulty and must not be used for protocol claims.

visualNotes: Recreates the original scanner-style idle, scanning, and found states using plain HTML/CSS/canvas.
Recreates a simple moon/space scanner visual in CSS/canvas instead of using the original lunar image or background assets.
Uses a placeholder candidate cat display derived from the found catId bytes; this is not original MoonCat parser rendering and must not be treated as a trait or image source.

knownRescuedCatIds.behavior: When supplied, valid candidate catIds that appear in the set are skipped and scanning continues until a valid unlisted candidate is found.
knownRescuedCatIds.labeling: The widget labels filtered results as valid unlisted candidates, not as currently rescuable cats.

notes: Defines the browser searchSeed.
Creates a miner with createMiner(searchSeed).
Receives mining result fields including catSeed and catId.
Calls mcr.rescueCat(catSeed) only after a candidate is chosen.
Strips a leading 0x from searchSeed before posting it to web workers.
Starts one worker per requested worker slot and stops workers when the first result returns.
Converts searchSeed hex to bytes.
Generates random 32-byte candidate seeds.
Hashes seed bytes concatenated with searchSeed bytes using keccak256.
Accepts the first hash with leading 000000.
Derives catId as 0x00 plus the final eight hex characters of the hash.
Inspected for scanner animation credits and UI visual context only.
Contains credits for a moon image URL and a 3d sphere mapping URL.
The widget preserves those credits in examples/rescue-mining-widget/README.md but does not copy the original lunar image or sphere mapping implementation.
Inspected for nearby scan/mining UI styling and local asset context.
The widget adapts the scanner-style panels, buttons, status flow, and space/moon visual direction with new CSS/canvas code.
The original image/font assets were not copied or referenced at runtime.
No repo-root LICENSE, COPYING, README, or package metadata file was present in the local mirror.
public/js/sha3.min.js identifies js-sha3 0.6.1 by Chen, Yi-Cyuan under MIT.
Only public/js/sha3.min.js was copied into examples/rescue-mining-widget/vendor/sha3.min.js from the original mirror.
The widget can use the vendored global js-sha3 keccak_256, accepts a caller-supplied keccak256 function, and keeps an internal Ethereum Keccak-256 fallback.
The KB already records the original contract rule that normal rescue cat IDs are derived from keccak256(seed, searchSeed) when the first three bytes of the hash are zero.

limitations: This is a compact reviewed summary and reusable snippet, not an import of the original website frontend.
Only public/scan.html, public/js/mine.js, and public/js/mine-worker.js were reviewed for browser rescue-mining behavior.
The snippet does not include wallet, private-key, provider, account, gas, transaction submission, or rescueCat calling code.
The embeddable widget does not include wallet, private-key, provider, account, gas, transaction submission, or rescueCat calling code.
No transaction is submitted and no network access is required.
The snippet and widget are source-bounded and contract-compatible, but they are not claimed to be byte-for-byte reproductions of the original browser worker or scanner UI.
The widget default difficultyPrefix is 000000 for original behavior; demo difficultyPrefix 0000 is only for faster local testing and is not original rescue difficulty.
The widget does not check current chain state and labels optional filtered results as valid unlisted candidates, not currently rescuable cats.
The widget does not include a rescued-cat dataset; knownRescuedCatIds must be supplied by the caller if filtering is desired.
The widget candidate cat display is a placeholder visualization derived from catId bytes, not original MoonCat parser rendering.
The original scanner animation credits were preserved, but the widget does not hotlink or copy the credited remote assets.
The widget vendors only public/js/sha3.min.js from the original mirror; Web3, workers, parser code, images, and unrelated frontend code remain excluded.
The reviewed mining behavior does not define image rendering, traits, parser behavior, rescue-order membership, ownership, or current availability of rescuable cats.
Random candidate generation was observed in the original browser worker; no seed increment behavior was observed in the reviewed files.
